title: Check a Realm Role in a Token
description:
## Does This Token Grant a Role?

Keycloak puts a user's realm roles in the access token under **`realm_access.roles`**. A resource server reads that claim to authorize requests.

A token is at **`/root/token.jwt`**.

**Your task:** decode it and decide whether it grants the **`admin`** realm role. Write **`granted`** (if `admin` is present) or **`denied`** (if not) to **`/root/role.txt`**.

> 💡 Decode the payload (as in the OIDC bundle), then test membership in `realm_access.roles`.

hints.title: Decode then test the roles array
hints.body: Parse the payload JSON and check whether 'admin' is in realm_access.roles.
hints.command: python3 -c "import base64,json; p=open('/root/token.jwt').read().strip().split('.')[1]; p+='='*(-len(p)%4); d=json.loads(base64.urlsafe_b64decode(p)); print('granted' if 'admin' in d.get('realm_access',{}).get('roles',[]) else 'denied')" > /root/role.txt

hints.title: With jq
hints.body: Decode the payload to JSON and use jq's index() to test membership.
hints.command: cut -d. -f2 /root/token.jwt | tr '_-' '/+' | base64 -d 2>/dev/null | jq -r 'if (.realm_access.roles | index("admin")) then "granted" else "denied" end'

validation.description: Checks that /root/role.txt reports the admin role as granted.
